raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/2024, I disputed a charge on my Citibank credit card for {$2100.00} that I believe was incorrect. Citi initially removed the charge, but later reinstated it without ever contacting me or providing any documentation or explanation. I was not notified by phone, email, or mail before the charge was added back. 

After discovering this reversal, I made multiple attempts to resolve the issue : I called Citis customer service numerous times and sent follow-up emails, but no one ever contacted me back. The only communication I received was from front-line call-center representatives who disconnected the call without transferring me to a resolution department or providing help. 

Because Citi failed to communicate or allow me to provide supporting documentation for my dispute, the account went delinquent and was ultimately charged off. This was entirely due to Citis mishandling of my dispute and lack of response. 

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and Regulation Z, a creditor is required to investigate disputed charges and communicate the results in writing. Citi did neither. I am requesting that this charge-off and all related negative credit reporting be removed or corrected to show paid as agreed, since I acted in good faith and was denied a fair opportunity to resolve the matter. 

I want the inaccurate charge-off status removed from all credit reports and corrected to reflect a positive or current standing. I also request a written confirmation of the dispute results and a direct explanation of how Citi investigated the original charge. Additionally, I want a written apology or acknowledgment for the repeated unreturned calls and emails.
